# Graph Snapshot Set Audit — 2026-04-29

**Status:** observation  
**Generated by:** Library Lane (verification-and-enforcement)  
**Evidence path:** `docs/graph/snapshots/`  
**Verified by:** self (runtime evidence from deployed API at deliberateensemble.works)

---

## 2. Federation Audit

**Interpretation status: observation**

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Nodes | 572 |
| Edges | 1,678 |
| Verified | 16 (2.8%) |
| Unverified | 551 (96.3%) |
| Conflicted | 5 (0.9%) |
| governanceLayer: unknown | 566 (98.9%) |
| authorityDepth: 0 | 572 (100%) |
| bridgeState: unknown | 561 (98.1%) |

### Federation Contradiction Hubs (5 nodes)

| Title | ContradictionCount | BridgeState | Status |
|-------|-------------------|-------------|--------|
| Federation | 4 | contradicted | CONFLICTED |
| THE COVENANT | 1 | contradicted | CONFLICTED |
| DESIGN PHILOSOPHY | 1 | contradicted | CONFLICTED |
| Layer 0: The Unifying Philosophy - The Gift | 1 | contradicted | CONFLICTED |
| The Vision: Persistent Multi-AI Collaboration Environment | 1 | contradicted | CONFLICTED |

### Federation Findings

1. **[observation]** 96.3% of Federation nodes are UNVERIFIED with governanceLayer=unknown, authorityDepth=0, bridgeState=unknown. The truth-routing mapper assigns these defaults when no cross-references or authority edges connect to a node.
2. **[hypothesis]** Federation's content is primarily game-engine/artifact content (`.gd`, `.tscn`, `.tres`) that lacks the semantic markers (tags, cross-refs, governance keywords) the truth-routing system uses for classification. This is a mapper classification gap, not a data integrity issue.
3. **[observation]** Federation has 1,678 edges despite having 0 cross-repo edges before the fix. Post-fix, cross-repo tag edge sampling connects Federation to other repos.
4. **[hypothesis]** The 5 conflicted nodes all have bridgeState=contradicted. These appear to be top-level governance/design documents that receive CONTRADICTS authority edges from other nodes referencing conflicting design directions.
5. **[observation]** Federation has 4 nodes with bridgeState=obsolete and 1 with bridgeState=verified — these are the only non-unknown/non-contradicted bridge states.

### Federation Action Items

- **Do not change mapper yet** — classify the under-classification as observation first
- Investigate whether `.gd`/`.tscn`/`.tres` files should receive automatic content_type classification (currently they fall to generic)
- Determine if game engine scenes should map to governanceLayer: application_adjacent by default
- Verify the 5 contradicted nodes are genuine contradictions vs. mapper false positives

---

## 3. SwarmMind Audit

**Interpretation status: observation**

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Nodes | 224 |
| Edges | 1,216 |
| Verified | 4 (1.8%) |
| Unverified | 218 (97.3%) |
| Conflicted | 2 (0.9%) |
| governanceLayer: unknown | 215 (96.0%) |
| governanceLayer: theoretical | 2 |
| governanceLayer: operational | 7 |
| bridgeState: unknown | 219 (97.8%) |

### SwarmMind Contradiction Hubs (2 nodes)

| Title | ContradictionCount | BridgeState | AuthorityDepth | Status |
|-------|-------------------|-------------|----------------|--------|
| THE SINGLE ENTRY POINT | 65 | contradicted | 80 | CONFLICTED |
| WE4FREE Publication Roadmap | 65 | contradicted | 80 | CONFLICTED |

### SwarmMind Findings

1. **[observation]** Both contradiction hubs have contradictionCount=65, identical to the same-titled nodes in kernel-lane, self-organizing-library, and other repos. This is the cross-lane shared-template pattern identified by kernel-lane.
2. **[hypothesis]** "THE SINGLE ENTRY POINT" appears as CONFLICTED with contradictionCount=65 in at least 4 repos (SwarmMind, kernel-lane, self-organizing-library, and likely Archivist-Agent). The identical count across repos strongly suggests this is a **shared-template/duplicate-title false positive** — the truth-routing mapper treats all files with the same title across repos as mutually contradicting, when in reality they are copies or variants of the same canonical governance text.
3. **[observation]** "WE4FREE Publication Roadmap" follows the same pattern: contradictionCount=65 across repos, suggesting it is also a shared-template false positive.
4. **[observation]** SwarmMind has 7 nodes with governanceLayer=operational and 2 with governanceLayer=theoretical — these are the only classified nodes out of 224.
5. **[observation]** authorityDepth=80 for the 2 conflicted nodes is anomalously high compared to most nodes (which have authorityDepth=0). This suggests the truth-routing system is creating deep derivation chains for these cross-referenced governance documents.

### SwarmMind Contradiction Classification

| Hub | Preliminary Classification | Reasoning |
|-----|---------------------------|-----------|
| THE SINGLE ENTRY POINT | duplicate-title/shared-template false positive | Same title, same contradictionCount across 4+ repos; likely copies of canonical governance text |
| WE4FREE Publication Roadmap | duplicate-title/shared-template false positive | Same pattern; publication roadmap replicated across repos |

### SwarmMind Action Items

- Verify the duplicate-title hypothesis by inspecting the actual content of "THE SINGLE ENTRY POINT" files across repos
- If confirmed as shared-template false positive, the mapper should de-duplicate cross-repo contradiction edges for identical-titled governance documents
- Do not change mapper yet — treat as hypothesis pending Library verification

| Named Failure Mode: Cross-Lane Observability Boundary | Library | 65 | under investigation |
| Failure Modes Index | Library | 65 | under investigation |

| Under investigation (needs content review) | ~4 | observation |
| True contradiction (unlikely from current data) | 0 | — |
| Stale historical | 0 | — |
| Unverified authority claim | 0 | — |

## 5. FreeAgent Classification Audit

**Interpretation status: observation**

FreeAgent has the highest density of contradiction hubs (64 nodes with contradictionCount > 0) and the highest node count (828).

| GovernanceLayer | Count |
|-----------------|-------|
| application_adjacent | (dominant, per kernel review) |
| theoretical | (secondary) |

FreeAgent's content spans multiple domains (game, medical, finance, topology, evidence, display) but the mapper collapses these into `application_adjacent` and `theoretical`. The kernel-lane review recommended separating these sub-classifications.

**Status:** observation — no mapper changes proposed yet.

---

## 6. Papers Audit

**Interpretation status: observation**

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Nodes | 5 |
| Edges | 52 |
| Verified | 0 |
| Unverified | 5 (100%) |
| Contradiction hubs | 0 |
| governanceLayer | unknown (all) |
| authorityDepth | 0 (all) |

### Papers Findings

1. **[observation]** Only 5 paper nodes exist, all UNVERIFIED, governanceLayer=unknown. Papers are indexed by filename only — sections, claims, and arguments within the PDFs are not represented as first-class graph nodes.
2. **[hypothesis]** Per kernel-lane review: "Papers mapping is too shallow" — paper sections/claims should be first-class nodes to enable meaningful graph traversal and contradiction detection across papers.
3. **[observation]** 52 edges connect the 5 paper nodes to other repos via shared-tag edges, suggesting cross-repo tag bridging is working for papers.

---

## 7. Snapshot Compare Utility

**Status:** implemented, baseline captured

The snapshot compare utility (`src/lib/graph-snapshot-compare.ts`) is live and wired to the UI. A programmatic snapshot generation script (`scripts/generate-snapshot-reports.js`) has been created for server-side report generation.

**Baseline snapshots** saved to `docs/graph/snapshots/`:
- Per-repo snapshots (9 files)
- Full graph snapshot (1 file)
- Per-repo contradiction hub reports (9 files)
- Full contradiction hub report (1 file)

**Compare reports** will be generated on the next run when previous snapshots exist for comparison.

### Next Steps for Compare Reports

1. Re-run `node scripts/generate-snapshot-reports.js` after any index regeneration to produce comparison data
2. Compare Federation snapshots across index regenerations
3. Compare SwarmMind snapshots across index regenerations
4. Document delta findings in follow-up audit docs
